author: Robert Lucier
title: Zoom on Nature with Coffee & Kim
summary: Kim Gaffett, The Nature Conservancy’s Naturalist on Block Island, will be hosting brief zoom calls focusing on the Island’s unique ecosystems, flora and fauna.  Locations change weekly!
content:
Kim Gaffett, The Nature Conservancy’s Naturalist on Block Island, will be hosting brief zoom calls focusing on the Island’s unique ecosystems, flora and fauna.  Locations change weekly!

June 29, 2020 @ 9:00 am – 10:00 am

Kim Gaffett, The Nature Conservancy’s Naturalist on Block Island, will be hosting brief zoom calls focusing on the Island’s unique ecosystems, flora and fauna.  Locations change weekly!

author: Cindy BlockIslandChamber
title: Farmers Market
summary: Visit the Block Island Farmer's Market for the latest arts and crafts on the island.
content:
Visit the Block Island Farmer's Market for the latest arts and crafts on the island.

If a drizzle of local honey on a fresh-baked scone is your idea of heaven, you can find heaven on earth on Wednesday and Saturday mornings from 9 to 11:30 am at the Block Island Farmers' Market. (Word to the wise, come early before the lemonade is all gone.)

With its many fruits and veggies, artisan cheese spreads, salsas, shellfish and more, the Market is a veritable festival for foodies. But wait, there's more. Not only are there plenty of edibles to delight your taste buds, there are also arts and crafts and music and kids and dogs and a laid-back community atmosphere that makes you smile just being there. 

Rain or shine, the Market is held this Saturday morning at the Spring House field.

Provided By: Block Island Tourism Council

meta.title: Farmers Market
meta.description: Visit the Block Island Farmer's Market for the latest arts and crafts on the island.

title: Farmers Market
summary: Visit the Block Island Farmer's Market for the latest arts and crafts on the island.
content:
Visit the Block Island Farmer's Market for the latest arts and crafts on the island.

If a drizzle of local honey on a fresh-baked scone is your idea of heaven, you can find heaven on earth on Wednesday and Saturday mornings from 9 to 11:30 am at the Block Island Farmers' Market. If it's hot, try Lynn's Lemonade for one of the most refreshing drinks on the island.

With its many fruits and veggies, artisan cheese spreads, salsas, shellfish and more, the Market is a veritable festival for foodies. But wait, there's more. Not only are there plenty of edibles to delight your taste buds, there are also arts and crafts and music and kids and dogs and a laid-back community atmosphere that makes you smile just being there. 

Rain or shine, the Market is held every Saturday morning at Legion Park. There’s also a Wednesday morning market at the Spring House Field, up Spring Street and behind the Spring House Hotel, through Labor Day.

Provided By: Block Island Tourism Council

author: Cindy BlockIslandChamber
title: Annual Thanksgiving Beach Walk to honor the Late Capt. John R. “Rob” Lewis
summary: Join us to celebrate this great man, who inspired so many  people to donate land and resources to support conservation on this beautiful island. (weather permitting)
content: Join us to celebrate this great man, who inspired so many  people to donate land and resources to support conservation on this beautiful island. (weather permitting)

author: Cindy BlockIslandChamber
title: Island Free Library - "Book, Bake, & Bloom"
summary: From 9 am to 1 pm, the library is abuzz with islanders and visitors looking for bargains on the book tables and blossoms on the plants outside.
content:
From 9 am to 1 pm, the library is abuzz with islanders and visitors looking for bargains on the book tables and blossoms on the plants outside.

And who can walk past all those decadent cakes, pies, cupcakes and cookies without giving in to temptation?

All those aromas of cinnamon and chocolate making you hungry? Take a break at the café for light fare like soup and sandwiches.

As in most small towns, the library is a year-round center of culture and entertainment with mahjong, cribbage, chess, crafts, knitting, book discussions, story times, even kids’ yoga classes. And this is your chance to support it all.

Provided By: Block Island Tourism Council

meta.title: Book Bake and Bloom
meta.description: Block Island Library annual book and plant sale.

author: Analyn Alforque
title: Annual Harbor Church Fair & Auction
summary: If you love scouring rummage sales and antique stores for unusual finds, you’ll probably want to stop by Harbor Church on Water Street for their Annual Church Fair & Auction on Saturday, July 27. It’s the major fundraiser for the church, and the money collected helps the church maintain its building for all to use, including about 30 community organizations.
content:
If you love scouring rummage sales and antique stores for unusual finds, you’ll probably want to stop by Harbor Church on Water Street for their Annual Church Fair & Auction on Saturday, July 27. It’s the major fundraiser for the church, and the money collected helps the church maintain its building for all to use, including about 30 community organizations.

Whether you’re on the lookout for baubles or books, antiques or art, you never know what you’ll find at the fair, so get there early to get first pick. 

The auction starts at 10:30 am.  Jazz Brunch at 9 am - 10:30 am - $10 at the church as well.

meta.title: Annual Harbor Church Fair & Auction
meta.description: Harbor Church on Water Street for their Annual Church Fair & Auction on Saturday, July 28.

address: Water St New Shoreham Washington County

author: Austin Crawford
title: 25th Annual Community Bird Census
summary: 1.  Meet at 9:30 a.m. at Sachem Pond
content:
1.  Meet at 9:30 a.m. at Sachem Pond

For duck viewing on Sachem Pond with a spotting scope & to start the day...

 

2. Bird Walk - 10a.m. - Location TBD

 

3. Collect Bird observations
Throughout the day observe at your own pace, from bird feeders, neighborhood walks, or farther afield.

 

4. Meet at 4:00 p.m. at TNC office, High St.
To compile a community bird list, enjoy cider and cookies, continue the Elizabeth Dickens-inspired tradition of making a bird census the day after Christmas, and revel in the stories of the 

 

For more information, or to submit your day's observations call/text Kim Gaffett at 401-595-7055. Or, email kimgaffett@gmail.com

author: Austin Crawford
title: Block Island Sound Baths at Spring Street Gallery
summary: Sound has an amazing affect on the human psyche. A sound bath is a meditative experience where you will be ‘bathed’ in ambient, relaxing sound waves.
content:
Sound has an amazing affect on the human psyche. A sound bath is a meditative experience where you will be ‘bathed’ in ambient, relaxing sound waves.   

All you have to do is relax and listen.

Yoga mats, blankets, pillows and silk eye covers will be provided.

Anyone is welcome to bring their own yoga mat or chair (or beanbag chair if you’re amazing).

Space is limited;  first come, first serve.

 

$25 for a 45 min sound bath. cash, check or venmo accepted

author: Austin Crawford
title: One-Day Wellness Experience with Ann Law
summary: ONE-DAY WELLNESS EXPERIENCE | BLOCK ISLAND
content:
ONE-DAY WELLNESS EXPERIENCE | BLOCK ISLAND
Start the New Year with a clear, simple plan for LASTING HEALTH

Join Ann Law, Global Wellness Expert and Bestselling Author, for a powerful one-day immersive experience focused on FOOD • FITNESS • FOCUS = HEALTH & FREEDOM
This hands-on experience blends education, nourishment, movement, and mindset to help you create sustainable habits that lead to renewed energy, ideal weight, and lifelong wellness
Saturday, January 17, 2026
10:00 AM – 6:00 PM
Block Island, Rhode Island
Investment: $149
Optional VIP upgrade available

Click the link for full details and registration https://lawofwellnessretreats.com/one-day

Spaces are limited for this intimate, in-person experience

title: Block Island School Friends Pie Sale at The 1661 Inn
summary: The Block Island School Friends are excited to announce our Annual Thanksgiving Pie Sale—our biggest fundraiser of the year! This year’s sale will take place at our new location, The 1661 Inn.  We’ll have a delicious assortment of homemade pies and baked goods, and as always, all proceeds directly benefit student activities and programs at the Block Island School. Get your favorites early—these pies go fast!

Get your favorites early—these pies go fast!

author: Austin Crawford
title: The 212 Job Fair
summary: JOB FAIR: With excitement and vision, The Martucci Group and the The 212 is hiring for our first summer season on Block Island! Be among the first to experience four new concepts under one roof, just 50 feet from the ferry:
content:
JOB FAIR: With excitement and vision, The Martucci Group and the The 212 is hiring for our first summer season on Block Island! Be among the first to experience four new concepts under one roof, just 50 feet from the ferry:

- Block Island Bunk House
- OneBI Steak & Oyster House
- Block Island ClamCake Co.
- Pasta Patch.

🏡Brand new luxurious employee housing in the heart of downtown, just a quick walk to work.  Flat Screen TVs / AC / Cafeteria / Laundry Services

We’re seeking out:

- Assistant General Manager
- Sous Chef
- Bartenders
- Hosts
- Line Cooks
- Bussers
- Dishwashers

Opportunities to advance your career in the restaurant industry.
We are looking forward to building a great team for this new adventure!

See flyer for details.

Want to interview virtually?
Apply today

 https://themartuccigroup.com/careers/

Questions? Email our GM @:

 Stephen@themartuccigroup.com

title: Earth Day Fair at Harbor Church
summary: Community Wide Earth Day Fair.  Stop by and check out what different Island Groups are doing to help protect the Island's environment.  There will be live music and food!
content:
Community Wide Earth Day Fair.  Stop by and check out what different Island Groups are doing to help protect the Island's environment.  There will be live music and food!

Participating Groups: The Nature Conservancy, Block Island Conservancy, The Solar Initiative, Harbor Baptist Church, Island Free Library, Low Season Artist Projects, Mystic Marine Animal Rescue, Harbors Dept, Coastal Resiliency Committee, Spar Point Farmer, RE Block Island, New Shoreham Utility District, Island Fiber Arts.
Weather dependent

author: Austin Crawford
title: Department of Taxation Compliance Workshop at Spring House Hotel
summary: Tax Compliance Day is an event hosted periodically by the Division of Taxation to give taxpayers an opportunity to seek resolution for your complex Rhode Island and/or federal tax issues in easily accessible locations and at convenient times.
content:
Tax Compliance Day is an event hosted periodically by the Division of Taxation to give taxpayers an opportunity to seek resolution for your complex Rhode Island and/or federal tax issues in easily accessible locations and at convenient times.

Tax Compliance Day events are intended to help taxpayers access representatives from the IRS, the RI Division of Taxation, and other state and federal agencies, all in the same location at the same time.

Upcoming Dates, times, locations, and who should attend:

  • May 15, 2025 – Block Island, RI
    • Tax Compliance Day for business entities & personal income tax filers
    • The Spring House Hotel, Victoria's Parlor
      52 Spring St
      New Shoreham, RI 02807
    • 10:00 am - 2:00 pm

Registration is free.  Registration is not required but it is encouraged.
Much more information and registration links are available at:
https://tax.ri.gov/about-us/tax-compliance-day

summary: Join us for a walk during peak goldenrod. Meet at Rodman's Hollow parking lot.
content:

Advance sign-up is recommended but not required. Email: kim.gaffett@tnc.org. 

 

 

The James Stover Block Island Exploration Series

Discover the fascinating nature of Block Island. Guided monthly walks for families (with kids of all ages) and individuals. Explore areas and species special to Block Island.  These walks are funded by friends of James Stover (1996-2003), in memory of a boy who loved exploring Block Island.

 

Programs are weather dependent

author: Austin Crawford
title: Food Safety Classes
summary: FOOD SAFETY CERTIFICATION/RECERTIFICATION CLASSES FOR 2024
content:
FOOD SAFETY CERTIFICATION/RECERTIFICATION CLASSES FOR 2024

 

CERTIFICATION/ CLASSES --- Registration fee of $205 includes textbook, study guide and examination for this 8 Hour Course. Textbook will be mailed to you prior to class.

 

RECERTIFICATION CLASSES --- New regulation requires everyone being recertified to take the exam. You have 3 choices:

 

  1. Exam only:  $95.00
  2. Review class, study guide and exam, no  textbook:  $170.00
  3. Textbook, study guide, review class and exam:  $205.00.  Textbook will be mailed to you prior to class.

Includes exam

 

Click the link to book now and pick June 9th.

https://www.foodsafety-certification.com/services

author: Austin Crawford
title: BICS Around the Island Pursuit Race
summary: This is a fun, scenic, around the island pursuit race, now in its 12th year. This Race is sailed under the rules of the ECSA and counts as a "points" race on their offshore racing circuit.
content:
This is a fun, scenic, around the island pursuit race, now in its 12th year. This Race is sailed under the rules of the ECSA and counts as a "points" race on their offshore racing circuit.

Registration is now open and available online via Regatta Network. Click the link below to begin your registration: https://www.regattanetwork.com/clubmgmt/applet_registration_form.php?regatta_id=27811

For race documents and more information click the link below. 

https://www.regattanetwork.com/clubmgmt/regatta_uploads/27811/2024AroundtheIslandPursuitRaceFlierV1.pdf

author: Austin Crawford
title: FREE pH soil testing and garden info
summary: A URI Master Gardener will be on hand for your gardening questions, and will be doing free pH soli testing at the Library this Thursday July 11
content:
A URI Master Gardener will be on hand for your gardening questions, and will be doing free pH soli testing at the Library this Thursday July 11

from 10:00-noon.


How to take a soil sample:

Dig out a cup of soil near root level (about 3-4 inches down in the soil.)

Remove any rocks, sticks or debris and let dry overnight in an open ziplock bag or paper bag.

Bring it to library and get a result as you wait!

author: Austin Crawford
title: Block Island Arts and Artisans Festival
summary: On the grounds of the Narragansett Inn, 1 Ocean Ave in New Harbor, Block Island. Across from Dead Eye Dick's.
content:
On the grounds of the Narragansett Inn, 1 Ocean Ave in New Harbor, Block Island. Across from Dead Eye Dick's.
Local artists and artisans will display their works for sale. You will find iconic Rhode Island paintings, Block Island photography, custom designed jewelry, children's appliquéd clothing, license plate art, blown and cut glass, fused glass, nostalgia art, seaweed prints, unique Block Island paintings, prints, ornaments, pottery, jewelry and so much more.

author: Austin Crawford
title: Walk to End Alzheimer's
summary: Come walk with us on Sunday September 10th.  Bring your friends, families, neighbors.  Help us find a cure for Alzheimer’s, a terrible disease that has touched many of us.  If walking isn’t your thing join us at the ceremony 11 am and cheer on
content:
Come walk with us on Sunday September 10th.  Bring your friends, families, neighbors.  Help us find a cure for Alzheimer’s, a terrible disease that has touched many of us.  If walking isn’t your thing join us at the ceremony 11 am and cheer on

If you wish to make a donation you can do so online at act.alz.org  (Block Island Walk Team Block Island) or a check made out to Alzheimer’s Association (Team Block Island written in the memo).   Local contact - Sandra Kelly POB 957.
